21.correct: The Right to Property was removed from the list of Fundamental Rights by the 44th Amendment (1978); it is now a legal right under Article 300A.

wrong.A: The Right to Education was added as a fundamental right, not removed.
wrong.C: Religious freedom remains a fundamental right.
wrong.D: The Right to Equality remains a fundamental right.

21.fact: The Right to Property is now an ordinary legal/constitutional right, not a fundamental right.

22.correct: Article 30 protects the right of minorities to establish and administer educational institutions of their choice.

wrong.A: Article 25 deals with freedom of conscience and religion.
wrong.B: Article 28 deals with religious instruction in institutions.
wrong.C: Article 29 protects the cultural and educational rights of minorities (but the right to establish institutions is Article 30).

22.fact: Articles 29 and 30 are the cultural and educational rights of minorities.

23.correct: The dhol is the instrument most prominently used in Bhangra music.

wrong.A: The tabla is used in classical music.
wrong.C: The sitar is a classical string instrument.
wrong.D: The violin is not central to Bhangra.

23.fact: The dhol's powerful beat drives the energetic Bhangra dance.

24.correct: Harper Lee's Pulitzer-winning novel 'To Kill a Mockingbird' deals with racial injustice in the American South.

wrong.A: 'Go Set a Watchman' is a related but separate Harper Lee work.
wrong.C: 'The Color Purple' was written by Alice Walker.
wrong.D: 'Invisible Man' was written by Ralph Ellison.

24.fact: 'To Kill a Mockingbird' (1960) is narrated by the child Scout Finch.

25.correct: A cataract in the human eye is usually corrected by surgery (replacing the clouded lens with an artificial one).

wrong.A: Eye drops alone do not cure a cataract.
wrong.B: Eye exercises do not remove a cataract.
wrong.D: Spectacles do not cure a cataract.

25.fact: A cataract is the clouding of the eye's natural lens, common with ageing.

26.correct: In target archery, an archer is generally given a maximum of 2 minutes to shoot three arrows (one end).

wrong.A: 1 minute is too short for three arrows.

26.fact: Archers shoot 'ends' of three or six arrows within set time limits.

27.correct: The Third Five-Year Plan is also called the 'Gadgil Yojana' (after D.R. Gadgil's role in planning).

wrong.A: The First Plan is not the Gadgil Yojana.
wrong.B: The Second Plan is the Mahalanobis Plan.
wrong.D: The Fourth Plan is not called the Gadgil Yojana.

27.fact: The Third Plan (1961-66) aimed at a self-reliant, self-generating economy.

28.correct: ATP stands for Adenosine triphosphate, the main energy currency of cells.

wrong.D: 'Adenine triphosphate' is incorrect; it is adenosine, not adenine.

28.fact: Energy is released when ATP is broken down into ADP and a phosphate group.

29.correct: Anil Kumble holds the record for the most Test wickets by an Indian bowler (619 wickets).

wrong.B: Harbhajan Singh took fewer Test wickets.
wrong.D: R. Ashwin's tally, while high, is the record being compared (Kumble leads among the listed).

29.fact: Anil Kumble once took all 10 wickets in a Test innings (vs Pakistan, 1999).

30.correct: The Bhakra-Nangal project is located in Himachal Pradesh and Punjab (on the Sutlej river).

wrong.A: It is not in Gujarat and Maharashtra.
wrong.C: It is not in Assam and West Bengal.
wrong.D: It is not in Odisha and Bihar.

30.fact: The Bhakra Dam is one of the highest gravity dams in India.

31.correct: Odisha's Subhadra Yojana provides women-centred financial assistance of Rs 50,000 (over 5 years) to eligible women.

wrong.A: It is not a child scholarship scheme.
wrong.B: It is not a student-loan scheme.
wrong.C: It is not a pension for men.

31.fact: Subhadra Yojana is a flagship women's welfare scheme of the Odisha government.

32.correct: NITI Aayog's Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) focuses on promoting innovation and entrepreneurship.

wrong.B: The Digital India initiative focuses on digital infrastructure.
wrong.C: The National Skill Development Mission focuses on skilling.
wrong.D: Startup India is a separate initiative (not a NITI Aayog programme).

32.fact: AIM set up Atal Tinkering Labs in schools and Atal Incubation Centres.

33.correct: As of July 2025, over 7.22 lakh pregnant women were registered as beneficiaries under Mission Poshan 2.0.

wrong.B: The food-fortification initiative is not the registration scheme here.
wrong.C: The Eat Right India Movement is a food-safety campaign.
wrong.D: PM Gati Shakti is an infrastructure scheme.

33.fact: Mission Poshan 2.0 (Saksham Anganwadi) targets malnutrition among children and mothers.

34.correct: In Magadha, the Nanda dynasty came after the Shishunaga dynasty.

wrong.A: The Gupta dynasty came much later.
wrong.C: The Maurya dynasty came after the Nandas.
wrong.D: The Haryanka dynasty came before the Shishunagas.

34.fact: The Nanda dynasty was overthrown by Chandragupta Maurya (with Chanakya's help).

35.correct: The SCAN portal, launched by Pralhad Joshi (Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution) in December 2024, stands for 'Subsidy Claim Application for NFSA' - a single-window portal for states to submit food-subsidy claims.

wrong.A: 'System for Credit Allocation Network' is not the correct expansion.
wrong.D: 'Smart Claims and Allocation Network' is incorrect.

35.fact: SCAN streamlines the submission and processing of food-subsidy claims under the National Food Security Act.

36.correct: India's Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) is a 4-stage rocket.
36.fact: The PSLV alternates solid and liquid propulsion across its four stages.

37.correct: The Young Bengal Movement was started by Henry Louis Vivian Derozio.

wrong.B: Keshab Chandra Sen was a Brahmo Samaj leader.
wrong.C: Raja Ram Mohan Roy founded the Brahmo Samaj.
wrong.D: A.O. Hume founded the Indian National Congress.

37.fact: Derozio inspired his students at Hindu College to question tradition and embrace rationalism.

38.correct: In 1951, about 17.3% of India's population lived in urban areas.
38.fact: India's urban population share has since risen to over 30%.

39.correct: In kabaddi, a raider gets a bonus point on crossing the bonus line when the defending (catcher) team has at least 6 players on the mat.
39.fact: The bonus line gives raiders an extra scoring opportunity when enough defenders are present.

40.correct: In the World Happiness Report 2024, Afghanistan was the least happy country in the world.

wrong.C: North Korea was not ranked there.
wrong.D: China was not the least happy.

40.fact: Afghanistan has ranked at or near the bottom of the happiness index in recent years.
